skill.name: frontend-agent
skill.description: Guides frontend development for the Unite-Hub Next.js app, including React components, routes, responsive layouts, shadcn/ui usage, and workspace-safe UI fixes.
skill.summary: Builds and fixes Unite-Hub frontend features with React, Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and shadcn/ui patterns.
skill.author: CleanExpo

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many high-risk keyword matches, but review shows most are frontend documentation examples and false positives. The real risks are legitimate but elevated: the skill guides file edits, local API calls, dynamic imports, and shell commands such as npm and npx. No evidence found of credential theft, malicious network exfiltration, weak cryptography implementation, or prompt injection instructions.

medium_findings.title: Package Installation and Dev Command Guidance
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill includes shell commands for shadcn package installation and starting a dev server. These are normal frontend tasks, but following them can change dependencies or run project code, so users should review commands before execution. Confidence: 0.72. Confidence reasoning: The commands are explicit and relevant to the skill, but no user-controlled command construction or malicious endpoint is present.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and relevant to the skill, but no user-controlled command construction or malicious endpoint is present.

medium_findings.title: Repository File Modification Workflow
medium_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE. The skill instructs the agent to read, create, and update frontend files and routes. This is expected for a coding skill, but it can affect application behavior and should run only in trusted repositories with normal review. Confidence: 0.68. Confidence reasoning: The modification workflow is clear, but it is aligned with the advertised purpose and does not show destructive behavior.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The modification workflow is clear, but it is aligned with the advertised purpose and does not show destructive behavior.

low_findings.title: Relative Local API Fetch Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for exfiltration. Fetch examples call relative application endpoints such as contact intelligence and email routes. No external domain, secret value, or credential exfiltration pattern was found. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: The network calls are visible and relative to the app, with no evidence of sending secrets to third parties.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network calls are visible and relative to the app, with no evidence of sending secrets to third parties.

low_findings.title: Dynamic Import Used for Next.js Performance
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The dynamic import example loads a local HeavyChart component through Next.js dynamic import for performance. No arbitrary module path, remote code loading, or runtime user input is shown. Confidence: 0.92. Confidence reasoning: The import target is a fixed local component path in a documented Next.js pattern.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The import target is a fixed local component path in a documented Next.js pattern.

low_findings.title: Crypto, Windows SAM, and Role Token Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The weak cryptography, Windows SAM, and LLM role token alerts appear to match substrings in design-system and UI guidance. No evidence found of credential store access, cryptographic implementation, or prompt-injection text that overrides evaluator instructions. Confidence: 0.90. Confidence reasoning: Review of the cited lines shows frontend metadata and design-system text, not security-sensitive operations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines shows frontend metadata and design-system text, not security-sensitive operations.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Keyword Matches Are File Search Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for reconnaissance. The find and grep commands locate frontend source files in the project tree. They do not collect host secrets, network details, environment data, or system identity. Confidence: 0.84. Confidence reasoning: The commands are scoped to project directories and file extensions used by the frontend workflow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are scoped to project directories and file extensions used by the frontend workflow.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Examples in Markdown
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents shell commands including find, grep, npm, and npx. They are legitimate frontend workflow commands but should be reviewed before execution. Confidence: 0.76. Confidence reasoning: The command examples are explicit, but there is no command injection or obfuscation.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit, but there is no command injection or obfuscation.

dangerous_patterns.title: Application Network Call Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes fetch examples that call local app API routes. These are not malicious, but network behavior is part of generated frontend code and should match the app security model. Confidence: 0.80. Confidence reasoning: The calls are clear relative endpoints, with no evidence of external exfiltration.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The calls are clear relative endpoints, with no evidence of external exfiltration.

content.value_statement: Frontend changes can drift from product standards and miss workspace safety rules. This skill guides React, Next.js, Tailwind, and shadcn/ui work for consistent Unite-Hub screens.
actual_capabilities: Guides React 19 and Next.js App Router component work.
Creates and updates dashboard routes, navigation, and breadcrumbs.
Applies Tailwind CSS, design tokens, and shadcn/ui conventions.
Adds loading, error, accessibility, and responsive UI states.
Checks workspace filtering patterns in frontend data access examples.
Provides task steps for common Unite-Hub frontend fixes.
limitations: It is tailored to the Unite-Hub project structure and design system.
It does not provide backend implementation details beyond API usage examples.
It may require manual review before running npm or npx commands.
It does not replace product design approval or accessibility testing.

use_cases.title: Fix Dashboard UI Defects
use_cases.description: Update broken layouts, empty states, buttons, and responsive behavior in dashboard pages.

use_cases.title: Create Product Routes
use_cases.description: Add new App Router pages with metadata, navigation entries, breadcrumbs, and consistent component structure.

use_cases.title: Standardize UI Patterns
use_cases.description: Align screens with Tailwind tokens, shadcn/ui components, loading states, and workspace-aware data access.

prompt_templates.title: Fix a Small UI Issue
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a focused visual bug.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-agent skill to fix the dashboard contact card spacing. Keep the existing design tokens and test mobile layout.

prompt_templates.title: Add a Dashboard Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a new route.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-agent skill to add an Analytics dashboard page with metadata, navigation, breadcrumbs, loading states, and responsive stat cards.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-agent skill to implement the Hot Leads send-email button with local API handling, toast feedback, error handling, and accessible controls.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Frontend Feature
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request for review and remediation.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the frontend-agent skill to review the Campaigns dashboard for workspace filtering, shadcn/ui consistency, responsive behavior, and missing error states.

output_examples.input: Fix the Contacts page layout on mobile.
output: Identifies the Contacts page and related components.
Updates responsive spacing, grid behavior, and empty states.
Reports the files changed and the viewport checks performed.

output_examples.input: Add an Analytics route to the dashboard.
output: Creates the new dashboard page and metadata.
Uses existing UI components and design tokens.

output_examples.input: Review frontend data isolation before launch.
output: Finds pages and components that query workspace data.
Flags missing workspace checks for review.
Suggests focused fixes and validation steps.

best_practices: Review design-system guidance before generating new components.
Keep API calls scoped to trusted local application routes.
Run dependency-changing commands only after reviewing their impact.
anti_patterns: Do not generate generic shadcn/ui defaults without project styling.
Do not skip workspace filtering when showing organization data.
Do not add interactive controls without loading, error, and accessibility states.

faq.question: What projects is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for the Unite-Hub Next.js frontend and its local component conventions.

faq.question: Can it create new pages?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides App Router page creation, metadata, navigation updates, and breadcrumbs.

faq.question: Does it use shadcn/ui?
faq.answer: Yes. It expects shadcn/ui components customized with project tokens and patterns.

faq.question: Does it make network calls?
faq.answer: It includes examples for relative local API calls inside the application.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill can guide file edits and shell commands, but no malicious intent was found.

faq.question: Should commands be reviewed before running?
faq.answer: Yes. Review npm, npx, and other shell commands before executing them in a repository.
